    }\n77\t    }\n78\t\n79\t    /// Build the default strand waterfall from config.\n80\t    ///\n81\t    /// The waterfall order is:\n82\t    /// Pluck → Mend → Explore → Weave → Unravel → Pulse → }\n211\t\n212\t    /// Run the waterfall, returning a `SelectOutcome` that carries the winning\n213\t    /// candidate (if any) plus restart diagnostics.\n214\t    ///\n215\t    /// Returns the full `Bead` (including its workspace path) so the caller\n216\t    /// can create the correct bead store for remote beads found by Explore.\n217\t    /// The accompanying `String` is the name of the strand that produced the\n218\t    /// candidate.\n219\t    ///\n220\t    /// When a strand returns `WorkCreated`, the waterfall restarts from Pluck.\n221\t    /// A restart cap prevents infinite loops (e.g. a strand that always creates\n222\t    /// work without producing a claimable bead).\n223\t    pub async fn select(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore, exclusions: &HashSet<BeadId>) -> Result<SelectOutcome> {\n224\t        const MAX_RESTARTS: u32 = 3;\n225\t        let mut restarts = 0u32;\n226\t        let mut restart_triggers: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n227\t\n228\t        'waterfall: loop {\n229\t            for strand in &self.strands {\n230\t                let start = Instant::now();\n231\t                let result = strand.evaluate(store).await;\n232\t                let elapsed_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n233\t\n234\t                match result {\n235\t                    StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n236\t                        // Filter out beads that are in the exclusion set (e.g.\n237\t                        // recently race-lost).  This prevents the waterfall from\n238\t                        // immediately re-selecting a bead that just lost a claim\n239\t                        // race to another worker.\n240\t                        let filtered: Vec<Bead> = beads\n241\t                            .into_iter()\n242\t                            .filter(|b| !exclusions.contains(&b.id))\n243\t                            .collect();\n244\t                        let excluded_count = beads.len().saturating_sub(filtered.len());\n245\t                        let _ = self\n246\t                            .telemetry\n247\t                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n248\t                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n249\t                                result: \"bead_found\".to_string(),\n250\t                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n251\t                            });\n252\t                        tracing::info!(\n253\t                            strand = strand.name(),\n254\t                            candidates = filtered.len(),\n255\t                            excluded = excluded_count,\n256\t                            elapsed_ms,\n257\t                            \"strand found candidates\"\n258\t                        );\n259\t                        if let Some(bead) = filtered.into_iter().next() {\n260\t                            return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n261\t                                bead: Some((bead, strand.name().to_string())),\n262\t                                waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n263\t                                restart_triggers,\n264\t                            });\n265\t                        }\n266\t                        continue;\n267\t                    }\n268\t                    StrandResult::WorkCreated => {\n269\t                        let _ = self\n270\t                            .telemetry\n271\t                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n272\t                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n273\t                                result: \"work_created\".to_string(),\n274\t                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n275\t                            });\n276\t                        restarts += 1;\n277\t                        restart_triggers.push(strand.name().to_string());\n278\t                        if restarts > MAX_RESTARTS {\n279\t                            tracing::warn!(\n280\t                                strand = strand.name(),\n281\t                                max_restarts = MAX_RESTARTS,\n282\t                                \"waterfall restart cap reached, continuing to evaluate remaining strands\"\n283\t                            );\n284\t                            // Do NOT return None — continue evaluating remaining strands\n285\t                            // so every strand emits telemetry and the operator can see\n286\t                            // why the worker is idle.\n287\t                            continue;\n288\t                        }\n289\t                        tracing::info!(\n290\t                            strand = strand.name(),\n291\t                            elapsed_ms,\n292\t                            restart = restarts,\n293\t                            \"strand created new work, restarting waterfall\"\n294\t                        );\n295\t                        continue 'waterfall;\n296\t                    }\n297\t                    StrandResult::NoWork => {\n298\t                        let _ = self\n299\t                            .telemetry\n300\t                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n301\t                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n302\t                                result: \"no_work\".to_string(),\n303\t                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n304\t                            });\n305\t                        tracing::info!(\n306\t                            strand = strand.name(),\n307\t                            elapsed_ms,\n308\t                            \"strand returned no work\"\n309\t                        );\n310\t                        continue;\n311\t                    }\n312\t                    StrandResult::Error(e) => {\n313\t                        let _ = self\n314\t                            .telemetry\n315\t                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n316\t                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n317\t                                result: \"error\".to_string(),\n318\t                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n319\t                            });\n320\t                        tracing::warn!(\n321\t                            strand = strand.name(),\n322\t                            error = %e,\n323\t                            elapsed_ms,\n324\t                            \"strand error, continuing to next strand\"\n325\t                        );\n326\t                        continue;\n327\t                    }\n328\t                }\n329\t            }\n330\t            // All strands evaluated without finding work or triggering a restart.\n331\t            return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n332\t                bead: None,\n333\t                waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n334\t                restart_triggers,\n335\t            });\n336\t        }\n337\t    }\n338\t\n339\t    /// Return the names of all configured strands (for telemetry/debugging).\n340\t    pub fn strand_names(&self) -> }\n590\t\n591\t    impl CountingStrand {\n592\t        fn no_work(\n593\t            name: &'static str,\n594\t            count: std::sync::Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32>,\n595\t        ) -> Self  fn work_created(\n604\t            name: &'static str,\n605\t            count: std::sync::Arc<std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32>,\n606\t        ) -> Self {\n607\t            CountingStrand {\n608\t StrandResult {\n622\t            self.count.fetch_add(1, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n623\t            if self.returns_work_created {\n624\t                StrandResult::WorkCreated\n625\t            } else {\n626\t                StrandResult::NoWork\n627\t            }\n628\t        }\n629\t    }\n630\t\n631\t    #[tokio::test]\n632\t    async fn restart_cap_still_evaluates_remaining_strands() {\n633\t        // When a strand repeatedly returns WorkCreated, the waterfall restarts.\n634\t        // After MAX_RESTARTS, the remaining strands should still be evaluated\n635\t        // so that operators see telemetry for every strand in the cycle.\n636\t        let creator_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n637\t        let observer_a_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n638\t        let observer_b_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n639\t\n640\t        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n641\t            Box::new(CountingStrand::work_created(\n642\t                \"creator\",\n643\t                creator_count.clone(),\n644\t            )),\n645\t            Box::new(CountingStrand::no_work(\n646\t                \"observer-a\",\n647\t                observer_a_count.clone(),\n648\t            )),\n649\t            Box::new(CountingStrand::no_work(\n650\t                \"observer-b\",\n651\t                observer_b_count.clone(),\n652\t            )),\n653\t        ]);\n654\t        let store = EmptyStore;\n655\t        let outcome = runner.select(&store).await.unwrap();\n656\t        assert!(outcome.bead.is_none());\n657\t\n658\t        // creator should have been evaluated MAX_RESTARTS + 1 = 4 times.\n659\t        assert_eq!(creator_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst), 4);\n660\t\n661\t        // After the restart cap, the remaining strands should each be evaluated\n662\t        // at least once (the final pass through the waterfall).\n663\t        let a = observer_a_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n664\t        let b = observer_b_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n665\t        assert!(\n666\t            a >= 1,\n667\t            \"observer_a should be evaluated at least once, got {a}\"\n668\t        );\n669\t        assert!(\n670\t            b >= 1,\n671\t            \"observer_b should be evaluated at least once, got {b}\"\n672\t        );\n673\t\n674\t        // All 4 WorkCreated events are captured in restart_triggers.\n675\t        assert_eq!(outcome.waterfall_restarts, 4);\n676\t        assert_eq!(outcome.restart_triggers.len(), 4);\n677\t        assert!(outcome.restart_triggers.iter().all(|t| t == \"creator\"));\n678\t    }\n679\t\n680\t    #[tokio::test]\n681\t    async fn Strand waterfall: ordered sequence of selection strategies.\n//!\n//! The StrandRunner evaluates strands in priority order. The first strand\n//! that yields a candidate wins. Strands are stateless — they receive queue\n//! state and return a candidate or nothing.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `types`, `config`, `bead_store`.\n\nmod explore;\nmod knot;\nmod mend;\nmod pluck;\npub mod pulse;\npub mod reflect;\npub mod splice;\npub mod unravel;\npub mod weave;\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::time::Instant;\n\nuse anyhow::Result;\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::config::Config;\nuse crate::types::{Bead, BeadId, StrandResult};\n\n/// Result of a single `StrandRunner::select()` call.\n///\n/// Carries both the winning candidate (if any) and diagnostic statistics\n/// about restarts that occurred during the waterfall — used to populate\n/// `worker.exhausted` telemetry with a per-iteration breakdown.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\npub struct SelectOutcome {\n    /// The candidate bead and the strand that found it, or `None` if\n    /// all strands returned `NoWork`.\n    pub bead: Option<(Bead, String)>,\n    /// How many times the waterfall restarted from Pluck (cap = `MAX_RESTARTS`).\n    pub waterfall_restarts: u32,\n    /// Names of strands that returned `WorkCreated` and triggered a restart.\n    /// Duplicate entries are preserved (one per restart event).\n    pub restart_triggers: Vec<String>,\n}\n\npub use explore::ExploreStrand;\npub use knot::KnotStrand;\npub use mend::{cleanup_orphaned_in_progress, MendStrand};\npub use pluck::PluckStrand;\npub use pulse::PulseStrand;\npub use reflect::{CliReflectAgent, ReflectAgent, ReflectStrand};\npub use splice::SpliceStrand;\npub use unravel::{UnravelAgent, UnravelStrand};\npub use weave::{CliWeaveAgent, WeaveAgent, WeaveStrand};\n\n/// A single selection strategy in the waterfall.\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\npub trait Strand: Send + Sync {\n    /// Human-readable name for telemetry.\n    fn name(&self) -> &str;\n\n    /// Evaluate this strand against the current queue state.\n    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult;\n}\n\n/// Runs strands in order, returning the first candidate found.\npub struct StrandRunner {\n    strands: Vec<Box<dyn Strand>>,\n    telemetry: crate::telemetry::Telemetry,\n}\n\nimpl StrandRunner {\n    pub fn new(strands: Vec<Box<dyn Strand>>) -> Self {\n        StrandRunner {\n            strands,\n            telemetry: crate::telemetry::Telemetry::new(\"strand-runner\".to_string()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Build the default strand waterfall from config.\n    ///\n    /// The waterfall order is:\n    /// Pluck → Mend → Explore → Weave → Unravel → Pulse → Reflect → Splice → Knot.\n    pub fn from_config(\n        config: &Config,\n        worker_id: &str,\n        registry: crate::registry::Registry,\n        telemetry: crate::telemetry::Telemetry,\n    ) -> Self {\n        let pluck = PluckStrand::new(\n            config.strands.pluck.exclude_labels.clone(),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        let heartbeat_dir = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let heartbeat_ttl = std::time::Duration::from_secs(config.health.heartbeat_ttl_secs);\n        let lock_dir = std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp\");\n        let log_dir = config\n            .telemetry\n            .file_sink\n            .log_dir\n            .clone()\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| config.workspace.home.join(\"logs\"));\n        let retention_days = config.telemetry.file_sink.retention_days;\n        let traces_dir = config.workspace.default.join(\".beads\").join(\"traces\");\n        let trace_retention_failed_days = config.strands.learning.trace_retention_failed_days;\n        let trace_retention_success_days = config.strands.learning.trace_retention_success_days;\n\n        // Create a new Registry instance pointing to the same path for ExploreStrand.\n        // We need to get the state_dir_for_explore before moving registry to MendStrand.\n        let state_fallback = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\");\n        let state_dir_for_explore = registry.path().parent().unwrap_or(&state_fallback);\n        let explore_registry = crate::registry::Registry::new(state_dir_for_explore);\n\n        let mend = MendStrand::new(\n            config.strands.mend.clone(),\n            heartbeat_dir,\n            heartbeat_ttl,\n            lock_dir,\n            worker_id.to_string(),\n            registry,\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            log_dir,\n            retention_days,\n            traces_dir,\n            trace_retention_failed_days,\n            trace_retention_success_days,\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            config.strands.learning.max_learnings,\n        );\n\n        let explore = ExploreStrand::new(\n            config.strands.explore.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            explore_registry,\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            worker_id.to_string(),\n        );\n\n        let state_base = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\");\n\n        let weave = WeaveStrand::new(\n            config.strands.weave.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            state_base.join(\"weave\"),\n            Box::new(CliWeaveAgent::new(config.agent.default.clone())),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        let unravel = UnravelStrand::new(\n            config.strands.unravel.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            state_base.join(\"unravel\"),\n            Box::new(unravel::CliUnravelAgent::new(config.agent.default.clone())),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        let pulse = PulseStrand::new(\n            config.strands.pulse.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            state_base.join(\"pulse\"),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        // Create the extraction agent if configured.\n        let reflect_agent = config\n            .strands\n            .reflect\n            .extraction_agent\n            .as_ref()\n            .map(|agent_cmd| {\n                Box::new(reflect::CliReflectAgent::new(\n                    agent_cmd.clone(),\n                    config.strands.reflect.extraction_prompt_template.clone(),\n                )) as Box<dyn reflect::ReflectAgent>\n            });\n\n        let reflect = ReflectStrand::new(\n            config.strands.reflect.clone(),\n            config.workspace.default.clone(),\n            state_base.join(\"reflect\"),\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            reflect_agent,\n        );\n\n        // Reconstruct heartbeat_dir for Splice (same path used by Mend).\n        let splice_heartbeat_dir = config.workspace.home.join(\"state\").join(\"heartbeats\");\n        let runner_telemetry = telemetry.clone();\n        let splice = SpliceStrand::new(\n            config.strands.splice.clone(),\n            splice_heartbeat_dir,\n            state_base.join(\"splice\"),\n            telemetry,\n        );\n\n        let knot = KnotStrand::new(config.strands.knot.clone(), runner_telemetry.clone());\n        StrandRunner {\n            strands: vec![\n                Box::new(pluck),\n                Box::new(mend),\n                Box::new(explore),\n                Box::new(weave),\n                Box::new(unravel),\n                Box::new(pulse),\n                Box::new(reflect),\n                Box::new(splice),\n                Box::new(knot),\n            ],\n            telemetry: runner_telemetry,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Run the waterfall, returning a `SelectOutcome` that carries the winning\n    /// candidate (if any) plus restart diagnostics.\n    ///\n    /// Returns the full `Bead` (including its workspace path) so the caller\n    /// can create the correct bead store for remote beads found by Explore.\n    /// The accompanying `String` is the name of the strand that produced the\n    /// candidate.\n    ///\n    /// When a strand returns `WorkCreated`, the waterfall restarts from Pluck.\n    /// A restart cap prevents infinite loops (e.g. a strand that always creates\n    /// work without producing a claimable bead).\n    pub async fn select(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore, exclusions: &HashSet<BeadId>) -> Result<SelectOutcome> {\n        const MAX_RESTARTS: u32 = 3;\n        let mut restarts = 0u32;\n        let mut restart_triggers: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\n        'waterfall: loop {\n            for strand in &self.strands {\n                let start = Instant::now();\n                let result = strand.evaluate(store).await;\n                let elapsed_ms = start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64;\n\n                match result {\n                    StrandResult::BeadFound(beads) => {\n                        // Filter out beads that are in the exclusion set (e.g.\n                        // recently race-lost).  This prevents the waterfall from\n                        // immediately re-selecting a bead that just lost a claim\n                        // race to another worker.\n                        let filtered: Vec<Bead> = beads\n                            .into_iter()\n                            .filter(|b| !exclusions.contains(&b.id))\n                            .collect();\n                        let excluded_count = beads.len().saturating_sub(filtered.len());\n                        let _ = self\n                            .telemetry\n                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n                                result: \"bead_found\".to_string(),\n                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                            });\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            strand = strand.name(),\n                            candidates = filtered.len(),\n                            excluded = excluded_count,\n                            elapsed_ms,\n                            \"strand found candidates\"\n                        );\n                        if let Some(bead) = filtered.into_iter().next() {\n                            return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n                                bead: Some((bead, strand.name().to_string())),\n                                waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n                                restart_triggers,\n                            });\n                        }\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    StrandResult::WorkCreated => {\n                        let _ = self\n                            .telemetry\n                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n                                result: \"work_created\".to_string(),\n                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                            });\n                        restarts += 1;\n                        restart_triggers.push(strand.name().to_string());\n                        if restarts > MAX_RESTARTS {\n                            tracing::warn!(\n                                strand = strand.name(),\n                                max_restarts = MAX_RESTARTS,\n                                \"waterfall restart cap reached, continuing to evaluate remaining strands\"\n                            );\n                            // Do NOT return None — continue evaluating remaining strands\n                            // so every strand emits telemetry and the operator can see\n                            // why the worker is idle.\n                            continue;\n                        }\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            strand = strand.name(),\n                            elapsed_ms,\n                            restart = restarts,\n                            \"strand created new work, restarting waterfall\"\n                        );\n                        continue 'waterfall;\n                    }\n                    StrandResult::NoWork => {\n                        let _ = self\n                            .telemetry\n                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n                                result: \"no_work\".to_string(),\n                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                            });\n                        tracing::info!(\n                            strand = strand.name(),\n                            elapsed_ms,\n                            \"strand returned no work\"\n                        );\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                    StrandResult::Error(e) => {\n                        let _ = self\n                            .telemetry\n                            .emit(crate::telemetry::EventKind::StrandEvaluated {\n                                strand_name: strand.name().to_string(),\n                                result: \"error\".to_string(),\n                                duration_ms: elapsed_ms,\n                            });\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            strand = strand.name(),\n                            error = %e,\n                            elapsed_ms,\n                            \"strand error, continuing to next strand\"\n                        );\n                        continue;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n            // All strands evaluated without finding work or triggering a restart.\n            return Ok(SelectOutcome {\n                bead: None,\n                waterfall_restarts: restarts,\n                restart_triggers,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Return the names of all configured strands (for telemetry/debugging).\n    pub fn strand_names(&self) -> Vec<&str> {\n        self.strands.iter().map(|s| s.name()).collect()\n    }\n}\n\n// ─── Unit tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::types::{Bead, BeadId};\n\n    /// A stub strand that always returns the given result.\n    struct StubStrand {\n        name: &'static str,\n        result: std::sync::Mutex<Option<StrandResult>>,\n    }\n\n    impl StubStrand {\n        fn no_work(name: &'static str) -> Self {\n            StubStrand {\n                name,\n                result: std::sync::Mutex::new(Some(StrandResult::NoWork)),\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn beads(name: &'static str, beads: Vec<Bead>) -> Self {\n            StubStrand {\n                name,\n                result: std::sync::Mutex::new(Some(StrandResult::BeadFound(beads))),\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn work_created(name: &'static str) -> Self {\n            StubStrand {\n                name,\n                result: std::sync::Mutex::new(Some(StrandResult::WorkCreated)),\n            }\n        }\n\n        fn error(name: &'static str, msg: &str) -> Self {\n            StubStrand {\n                name,\n                result: std::sync::Mutex::new(Some(StrandResult::Error(\n                    crate::types::StrandError::ConfigError(msg.to_string()),\n                ))),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[async_trait::async_trait]\n    impl Strand for StubStrand {\n        fn name(&self) -> &str {\n            self.name\n        }\n\n        async fn evaluate(&self, _store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n            self.result\n                .lock()\n                .unwrap()\n                .take()\n                .unwrap_or(StrandResult::NoWork)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_test_bead(id: &str) -> Bead {\n        use chrono::Utc;\n        Bead {\n            id: BeadId::from(id.to_string()),\n            title: format!(\"Test bead {id}\"),\n            body: None,\n            priority: 1,\n            status: crate::types::BeadStatus::Open,\n            assignee: None,\n            labels: vec![],\n            workspace: std::path::PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n            dependencies: vec![],\n            dependents: vec![],\n std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n            if self.returns_work_created {\n                StrandResult::WorkCreated\n            } else {\n                StrandResult::NoWork\n std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n        let observer_a_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n        let observer_b_count = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU32::new(0));\n\n        let runner = StrandRunner::new(vec![\n            Box::new(CountingStrand::work_created(\n                \"creator\",\n                creator_count.clone(),\n            )),\n            Box::new(CountingStrand::no_work(\n                \"observer-a\",\n                observer_a_count.clone(),\n            )),\n runner.select(&store).await.unwrap();\n        assert!(outcome.bead.is_none());\n\n        // creator should have been evaluated MAX_RESTARTS + 1 = 4 times.\n        assert_eq!(creator_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst), 4);\n\n        // After the restart cap, the remaining strands should each be evaluated\n        // at least once (the final pass through the waterfall).\n        let a = observer_a_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n        let b = observer_b_count.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::SeqCst);\n        assert!(\n 4);\n        assert!(outcome.restart_triggers.iter().all(|t| t == \"creator\"));\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn empty_bead_found_continues_to_next() {\n        let bead = {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_cb7ff2d3a6bf4f4d94d658f2","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Mend strand: maintenance and self-healing.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Strand 2 in the waterfall. Runs after Pluck finds no work. Cleans up\n4\t//! stale claims, orphaned locks, broken dependency links, and database\n5\t//! corruption. If any cleanup is performed, returns `WorkCreated` so the\n6\t//! waterfall restarts from Pluck (released beads may now be claimable).\n7\t//!\n8\t//! Depends on: `bead_store`, `config`, `health`, `peer`, `registry`,\n9\t//!             `telemetry`, `types`, `trace`.\n10\t\n11\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n12\tuse std::time::{Duration, SystemTime};\n13\t\n14\tuse anyhow::Result;\n15\t\n16\tuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\n17\tuse crate::config::MendConfig;\n18\tuse crate::health::HealthMonitor;\n19\tuse crate::learning::LearningsFile;\n20\tuse crate::peer::PeerMonitor;\n21\tuse crate::registry::Registry;\n22\tuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\n23\tuse crate::trace::cleanup_traces;\n24\tuse crate::types::{BeadStatus, StrandError, StrandResult};\n25\t\n26\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n27\t// Store-scoped orphan cleanup (shared between Mend and Explore)\n28\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n29\t\n30\t/// Scan all in-progress beads in a store and release any whose assignee does\n31\t/// not correspond to a live worker. This catches beads orphaned by workers\n32\t/// that died without leaving a heartbeat file (or whose heartbeat was already\n33\t/// cleaned up).\n34\t///\n35\t/// This is a store-scoped function that can be called against any BeadStore,\n36\t/// not just the home workspace. Used by both MendStrand (for home) and\n37\t/// ExploreStrand (for remote workspaces).\n38\t///\n39\t/// # Arguments\n40\t/// * `store` - The bead store to scan (can be home or remote)\n41\t/// * `registry` - Worker registry for live worker lookup\n42\t/// * `telemetry` - Telemetry emitter for orphan release events\n43\t/// * `qualified_id` - This worker's fully-qualified identity (excluded from orphan detection)\n44\t///\n45\t/// # Returns\n46\t/// * `Ok(u32)` - Number of orphans released\n47\t/// * `Err(anyhow::Error)` - Store read failure\n48\tpub async fn cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n49\t    store: &dyn BeadStore,\n50\t    registry: &Registry,\n51\t    telemetry: &Telemetry,\n52\t    qualified_id: &str,\n53\t) -> Result<u32> {\n54\t    let all_beads = store.list_all().await?;\n55\t    let workers = registry.list()?;\n56\t\n57\t    // Build a set of fully-qualified worker IDs for registered, alive workers.\n58\t    let live_worker_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = workers\n59\t        .iter()\n60\t        .filter(|w| HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(w.pid))\n61\t        .map(|w| w.id.clone())\n62\t        .collect();\n63\t\n64\t    let mut released = 0u32;\n65\t\n66\t    for bead in &all_beads {\n67\t        if bead.status != BeadStatus::InProgress {\n68\t            continue;\n69\t        }\n70\t\n71\t        let assignee = match &bead.assignee {\n72\t            Some(a) if !a.is_empty() => a,\n73\t            _ => continue,\n74\t        };\n75\t\n76\t        // Skip if the assignee matches our own qualified identity (we're running).\n77\t        if assignee == qualified_id {\n78\t            continue;\n79\t        }\n80\t\n81\t        // Skip if the assignee matches a registered, alive worker.\n82\t        // Workers register with fully-qualified IDs ({adapter}-{worker_id}),\n83\t        // so this comparison prevents collisions when workers from different\n84\t        // adapter pools share a NATO name.\n85\t        if live_worker_ids.contains(assignee.as_str()) {\n86\t            continue;\n87\t        }\n88\t\n89\t        // Orphaned: assignee is not a live registered worker. Release it.\n90\t        tracing::info!(\n91\t            bead_id = %bead.id,\n92\t            assignee = %assignee,\n93\t            workspace = %bead.workspace.display(),\n94\t            \"releasing orphaned in-progress bead (assignee has no live worker)\"\n95\t        );\n96\t\n97\t        match store.release(&bead.id).await {\n98\t            Ok(()) => {\n99\t                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckReleased {\n100\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n101\t                    peer_worker: assignee.clone(),\n102\t                });\n103\t                released += 1;\n104\t            }\n105\t            Err(e) => {\n106\t                tracing::warn!(\n107\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n108\t                    error = %e,\n109\t                    \"failed to release orphaned in-progress bead\"\n110\t                );\n111\t            }\n112\t        }\n113\t    }\n114\t\n115\t    Ok(released)\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t/// Summary of work performed during one Mend evaluation cycle.\n119\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n120\tstruct MendSummary {\n121\t    beads_released: u32,\n122\t    locks_removed: u32,\n123\t    deps_cleaned: u32,\n124\t    db_repaired: bool,\n125\t    db_rebuilt: bool,\n126\t    agent_logs_cleaned: u32,\n127\t    traces_pruned: u32,\n128\t    traces_cleaned: u32,\n129\t    learnings_pruned: u32,\n130\t    learnings_consolidated: u32,\n131\t}\n132\t\n133\timpl MendSummary {\n134\t    /// Whether mend performed work that changes bead store state.\n135\t    ///\n136\t    /// Only operations that affect what Pluck finds on the next waterfall pass\n137\t    /// should return true: releasing beads, removing blocking locks, fixing\n138\t    /// stale dependency links, repairing/rebuilding the DB.\n139\t    ///\n140\t    /// Maintenance operations (log cleanup, trace pruning, learning\n141\t    /// consolidation) are housekeeping — they don't add or release claimable\n142\t    /// beads and must NOT trigger a waterfall restart.\n143\t    fn did_work(&self) -> bool {\n144\t        self.beads_released > 0\n145\t            || self.locks_removed > 0\n146\t            || self.deps_cleaned > 0\n147\t            || self.db_repaired\n148\t            || self.db_rebuilt\n149\t    }\n150\t}\n151\t\n152\t/// The Mend strand — maintenance and self-healing.\n153\tpub struct MendStrand {\n154\t    config:    Ok(())\n246\t    }\n247\t\n248\t    // ── Step 1.5: Orphaned in-progress bead recovery ────────────────────────\n249\t\n250\t    /// Scan all in-progress beads and release any whose assignee does     .await?;\n266\t        summary.beads_released += released;\n267\t        Ok(())\n268\t    }\n269\t\n270\t    // ── Step 2: Orphaned lock file removal ───────────────────────────────────\n271\t\n272\t    /// Remove claim lock files that are older than the configured TTL and not\n273\t    /// actively held by any process.\n274\t    fn cleanup_orphaned_locks(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n275\t        let lock_ttl = Duration::from_secs(self.config.lock_ttl_secs);\n276\t\n277\t        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.lock_dir) {\n278\t            Ok(e) => e,\n279\t            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n280\t            Err(e) => {\n281\t                tracing::warn!(\n282\t                    dir = %self.lock_dir.display(),\n283\t                    error = %e,\n284\t                    \"failed to read lock directory\"\n285\t                );\n286\t                return Ok(());\n287\t            }\n288\t        };\n289\t\n290\t        for entry in entries {\n291\t            let entry = match entry {\n292\t                Ok(e) => e,\n293\t                Err(_) => continue,\n294\t            };\n295\t\n296\t            let path = entry.path();\n297\t            let name = match path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {\n298\t                Some(n) => n,\n299\t                None => continue,\n300\t            };\n301\t\n302\t            // Only consider needle claim lock files.\n303\t            if !name.starts_with(\"needle-claim-\") || !name.ends_with(\".lock\") {\n304\t                continue;\n305\t            }\n306\t\n307\t            // Check file age.\n308\t            let age = match file_age(&path) {\n309\t                Some(age) => age,\n310\t                None => continue,\n311\t            };\n312\t\n313\t            if age <= lock_ttl {\n314\t                continue;\n315\t            }\n316\t\n317\t            // Try to acquire flock (non-blocking). If we can acquire it,\n318\t            // no one is holding it — safe to delete.\n319\t            match try_acquire_flock(&path) {\n320\t                Ok(Some(_file)) => {\n321\t                    // Lock acquired — no one holds it. Remove the file.\n322\t                    let age_secs = age.as_secs();\n323\t                    if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(&path) {\n324\t                        tracing::warn!(\n325\t                            path = %path.display(),\n326\t                            error = %e,\n327\t                            \"failed to remove orphaned lock file\"\n328\t                        );\n329\t                        continue;\n330\t                    }\n331\t\n332\t                    tracing::info!(\n333\t                        path = %path.display(),\n334\t                        age_secs,\n335\t                        \"removed orphaned lock file\"\n336\t                    );\n337\t\n338\t                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendOrphanedLockRemoved {\n339\t                        lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n340\t                        age_secs,\n341\t                    });\n342\t\n343\t                    summary.locks_removed += 1;\n344\t                }\n345\t                Ok(None) => {\n346\t                    // Lock is actively held — skip.\n347\t                    tracing::debug!(\n348\t                        path = %path.display(),\n349\t                        \"lock file is actively held, skipping\"\n350\t                    );\n351\t                }\n352\t                Err(e) => {\n353\t                    tracing::debug!(\n354\t                        path = %path.display(),\n355\t                        error = %e,\n356\t                        \"failed to probe lock file\"\n357\t                    );\n358\t                }\n359\t            }\n360\t        }\n361\t\n362\t        Ok(())\n363\t    }\n364\t\n365\t    // ── Step 3: Dependency link repair ───────────────────────────────────────\n366\t\n367\t    /// Find open beads that have closed blockers and clean up the stale\n368\t    /// dependency links.\n369\t    ///\n370\t    /// br does not automatically resolve dependency links on bead closure,\n371\t    /// so a bead can remain blocked even after its blocker is closed.\n372\t    async fn cleanup_stale_dependencies(\n373\t        &self,\n374\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n375\t        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n376\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n377\t        let all_beads = store.list_all().await?;\n378\t\n379\t        for bead in &all_beads {\n380\t            // Only check open beads that have dependencies.\n381\t            if bead.status != BeadStatus::Open || bead.dependencies.is_empty() {\n382\t                continue;\n383\t            }\n384\t\n385\t            for dep in &bead.dependencies {\n386\t                // Only check \"blocks\" type dependencies where the blocker is closed.\n387\t                if dep.dependency_type != \"blocks\" {\n388\t                    continue;\n389\t                }\n390\t\n391\t                if dep.status == \"closed\" {\n392\t                    tracing::info!(\n393\t                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n394\t                        blocker_id = %dep.id,\n395\t                        \"found stale dependency link (closed blocker on open bead)\"\n396\t                    );\n397\t\n398\t                    // Emit telemetry for the cleaned dependency.\n399\t                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendDependencyCleaned {\n400\t                        bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n401\t                        blocker_id: dep.id.clone(),\n402\t                    });\n403\t\n404\t                    summary.deps_cleaned += 1;\n405\t                }\n406\t            }\n407\t        }\n408\t\n409\t        Ok(())\n410\t    }\n411\t\n412\t    // ── Step 4: Agent log file cleanup ──────────────────────────────────────\n413\t\n414\t    /// Delete `.agent.jsonl` files in `log_dir` that are older than\n415\t    /// `retention_days` and do not belong to a currently-executing bead.\n416\t    ///\n417\t    /// Files are matched by the `.agent.jsonl` suffix. The bead ID is parsed\n418\t    /// from the stem (`<worker>-<bead>`): a file is considered active if the\n419\t    /// stem ends with `-<bead_id>` for any in-progress bead.\n420\t    async fn cleanup_old_agent_logs(\n421\t        &self,\n422\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n423\t        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n424\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n425\t        if self.retention_days == 0 {\n426\t            return Ok(());\n427\t        }\n428\t\n429\t        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.log_dir) {\n430\t            Ok(e) => e,\n431\t            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n432\t            Err(e) => {\n433\t                tracing::warn!(\n434\t                    dir = %self.log_dir.display(),\n435\t                    error = %e,\n436\t                    \"mend: failed to read log directory for agent log cleanup\"\n437\t                );\n438\t                return Ok(());\n439\t            }\n440\t        };\n441\t\n442\t        // Collect all .agent.jsonl paths first so we can bail early if none.\n443\t        let agent_log_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = entries\n444\t            .filter_map(|e| e.ok())\n445\t            .map(|e| e.path())\n446\t            .filter(|p| {\n447\t                p.file_name()\n448\t                    .and_then(|n| n.to_str())\n449\t                    .map(|n| n.ends_with(\".agent.jsonl\"))\n450\t                    .unwrap_or(false)\n451\t            })\n452\t            .collect();\n453\t\n454\t        if agent_log_paths.is_empty() {\n455\t            return Ok(());\n456\t        }\n457\t\n458\t        let cutoff = Duration::from_secs(u64::from(self.retention_days) * 86400);\n459\t\n460\t        // Build the set of currently in-progress bead IDs.\n461\t        let in_progress_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = store\n462\t            .list_all()\n463\t            .await?\n464\t            .into_iter()\n465\t            .filter(|b| b.status == BeadStatus::InProgress)\n466\t            .map(|b| b.id.as_ref().to_string())\n467\t            .collect();\n468\t\n469\t        for path in &agent_log_paths {\n470\t            // Check file age.\n471\t            let age = match file_age(path) {\n472\t                Some(a) => a,\n473\t                None => continue,\n474\t            };\n475\t\n476\t            if age <= cutoff {\n477\t                continue;\n478\t            }\n479\t\n480\t            // Skip if this log belongs to an in-progress bead.\n481\t            let stem = match path\n482\t                .file_name()\n483\t                .and_then(|n| n.to_str())\n484\t                .and_then(|n| n.strip_suffix(\".agent.jsonl\"))\n485\t            {\n486\t                Some(s) => s.to_string(),\n487\t                None => continue,\n488\t            };\n489\t\n490\t            let is_active = in_progress_ids\n491\t                .iter()\n492\t                .any(|bead_id| stem == *bead_id || stem.ends_with(&format!(\"-{bead_id}\")));\n493\t\n494\t            if is_active {\n495\t                tracing::debug!(\n496\t                    path = %path.display(),\n497\t                    \"skipping agent log for in-progress bead\"\n498\t                );\n499\t                continue;\n500\t            }\n501\t\n502\t            if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(path) {\n503\t                tracing::warn!(\n504\t                    path = %path.display(),\n505\t                    error = %e,\n506\t                    \"mend: failed to remove stale agent log\"\n507\t                );\n508\t                continue;\n509\t            }\n510\t\n511\t            tracing::info!(\n512\t                path = %path.display(),\n513\t                age_secs = age.as_secs(),\n514\t                \"mend: removed stale agent log\"\n515\t            );\n516\t            summary.agent_logs_cleaned += 1;\n517\t        }\n518\t\n519\t        Ok(())\n520\t    }\n521\t\n522\t    // ── Step 4.5: Trace retention cleanup ───────────────────────────────────────\n523\t\n524\t    /// Clean up old traces based on retention policy.\n525\t    ///\n526\t    /// - Failed beads (non-zero exit): delete after trace_retention_failed_days\n527\t    /// - Successful beads (exit 0): prune data after trace_retention_success_days, keep metadata only\n528\t    fn cleanup_old_traces(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n529\t        if !self.traces_dir.exists() {\n530\t            return Ok(());\n531\t        }\n532\t\n533\t        match cleanup_traces(\n534\t            &self.traces_dir,\n535\t            self.trace_retention_failed_days,\n536\t            self.trace_retention_success_days,\n537\t        ) {\n538\t            Ok(cleanup_summary) => {\n539\t                summary.traces_pruned = cleanup_summary.traces_pruned;\n540\t                summary.traces_cleaned = cleanup_summary.traces_deleted;\n541\t\n542\t                if cleanup_summary.traces_pruned > 0 || cleanup_summary.traces_deleted > 0 {\n543\t                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendTraceCleanup {\n544\t                        traces_pruned: cleanup_summary.traces_pruned,\n545\t                        traces_deleted: cleanup_summary.traces_deleted,\n546\t                    });\n547\t\n548\t                    tracing::info!(\n549\t                        traces_pruned = cleanup_summary.traces_pruned,\n550\t                        traces_deleted = cleanup_summary.traces_deleted,\n551\t                        \"mend: cleaned up old traces\"\n552\t                    );\n553\t                }\n554\t            }\n555\t            Err(e) => {\n556\t                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: trace cleanup failed\");\n557\t            }\n558\t        }\n559\t\n560\t        Ok(())\n561\t    }\n562\t\n563\t    // ── Step 4.75: Learning consolidation ───────────────────────────────────────\n564\t\n565\t    /// Clean up and consolidate workspace learnings.\n566\t    ///\n567\t    /// 1. Prune stale entries (>90 days without reinforcement)\n568\t    /// 2. Consolidate if entries exceed max_learnings\n569\t    fn cleanup_learnings(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n570\t        let mut learnings = match LearningsFile::load(&self.workspace) {\n571\t            Ok(l) => l,\n572\t            Err(e) => {\n573\t                tracing::debug!(error = %e, \"mend: no learnings file to clean up\");\n574\t                return Ok(());\n575\t            }\n576\t        };\n577\t\n578\t        let _original_count = learnings.entries().len();\n579\t\n580\t        // Step 1: Prune stale entries\n581\t        match learnings.prune_stale() {\n582\t            Ok(pruned) => {\n583\t                if pruned > 0 {\n584\t                    tracing::info!(pruned, \"mend: pruned stale learning entries\");\n585\t                    summary.learnings_pruned = pruned as u32;\n586\t                }\n587\t            }\n588\t            Err(e) => {\n589\t                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: failed to prune stale learnings\");\n590\t            }\n591\t        }\n592\t\n593\t        // Step 2: Consolidate if over limit\n594\t        if learnings.entries().len() > self.max_learnings {\n595\t            match learnings.consolidate(self.max_learnings) {\n596\t                Ok(removed) => {\n597\t                    if removed > 0 {\n598\t                        tracing::info!(\n599\t                            removed,\n600\t                            max_count = self.max_learnings,\n601\t                            \"mend: consolidated learning entries\"\n602\t                        );\n603\t                        summary.learnings_consolidated = removed as u32;\n604\t                    }\n605\t                }\n606\t                Err(e) => {\n607\t                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: failed to consolidate learnings\");\n608\t                }\n609\t            }\n610\t        }\n611\t\n612\t        if summary.learnings_pruned > 0 || summary.learnings_consolidated > 0 {\n613\t            let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendLearningCleanup {\n614\t                pruned: summary.learnings_pruned,\n615\t                consolidated: summary.learnings_consolidated,\n616\t            });\n617\t        }\n618\t\n619\t        Ok(())\n620\t    }\n621\t\n622\t    // ── Step 5: Database health check with auto-recovery ────────────────────\n623\t\n624\t    /// Run `br doctor` to check database health. If issues are found,\n625\t    /// escalate through the recovery pipeline:\n626\t    ///\n627\t    /// 1. `br doctor` (check only) → if warnings found:\n628\t    /// 2. `br doctor --repair` → if fails:\n629\t    /// 3. Full rebuild (rm .beads/beads.db + br sync --import + verify)\n630\t    ///\n631\t    /// If the full rebuild also fails, the JSONL itself may be corrupt.\n632\t    async fn check_db_health(\n633\t        &self,\n634\t        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n635\t        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n636\t    ) -> Result<()> {\n637\t        // Step 1: Probe with `br doctor` (no repair).\n638\t        let check_result = store.doctor_check().await;\n639\t\n640\t        let needs_repair = match check_result {\n641\t            Ok(report) => {\n642\t                // Doctor succeeded. If there are warnings, escalate to repair.\n643\t                !report.warnings.is_empty()\n644\t            }\n645\t            Err(e) => {\n646\t                let msg = format!(\"{e:#}\");\n647\t                if crate::bead_store::is_corruption_error(&msg) {\n648\t                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"br doctor detected corruption\");\n649\t                    true\n650\t                } else {\n651\t                    // Non-corruption error (e.g., br not found). Propagate.\n652\t                    return Err(e);\n653\t                }\n654\t            }\n655\t        };\n656\t\n657\t        if !needs_repair {\n658\t            return Ok(());\n659\t        }\n660\t\n661\t        // Step 2: Try `br doctor --repair`.\n662\t        tracing::info!(\"database health check found issues, attempting repair\");\n663\t        match store.doctor_repair().await {\n664\t            Ok(report) => {\n665\t                let warnings = report.warnings.len() as u32;\n666\t                let fixed = report.fixed.len() as u32;\n667\t                tracing::info!(warnings, fixed, \"br doctor --repair completed\");\n668\t\n669\t                let _ = self\n670\t                    .telemetry\n671\t                    .emit(EventKind::MendDbRepaired { warnings, fixed });\n672\t\n673\t                summary.db_repaired = true;\n674\t                return Ok(());\n675\t            }\n676\t            Err(e) => {\n677\t                tracing::warn!(\n678\t                    error = %e,\n679\t                    \"br doctor --repair failed, attempting full rebuild\"\n680\t                );\n681\t            }\n682\t        }\n683\t\n684\t        // Step 3: Full rebuild — rm db + br sync --import + verify.\n685\t        match store.full_rebuild().await {\n686\t            Ok(()) => {\n687\t                tracing::info!(\"database fully rebuilt from JSONL\");\n688\t\n689\t                let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendDbRebuilt);\n690\t\n691\t                summary.db_rebuilt = true;\n692\t                Ok(())\n693\t            }\n694\t            Err(e) => {\n695\t                tracing::error!(\n696\t                    error = %e,\n697\t                    \"full database rebuild failed — JSONL may be corrupt\"\n698\t                );\n699\t                Err(e)\n700\t            }\n701\t        }\n702\t    }\n703\t}\n704\t\n705\t#[async_trait::async_trait]\n706\timpl super::Strand for MendStrand {\n707\t    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n708\t        \"mend\"\n709\t    }\n710\t\n711\t    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n712\t        let mut summary = MendSummary::default();\n713\t\n714\t        // Step 1: Stale claim cleanup via peer monitoring.\n715\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_stale_claims(store, &mut summary).await {\n716\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: stale claim cleanup failed\");\n717\t            return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n718\t        }\n719\t\n720\t        // Step 1.5: Orphaned in-progress bead recovery (registry-based).\n721\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(store, &mut summary).await {\n722\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: orphaned in-progress cleanup failed\");\n723\t            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n724\t        }\n725\t\n726\t        // Step 2: Orphaned lock file removal.\n727\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_orphaned_locks(&mut summary) {\n728\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: orphaned lock cleanup failed\");\n729\t            return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n730\t        }\n731\t\n732\t        // Step 3: Dependency link repair.\n733\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_stale_dependencies(store, &mut summary).await {\n734\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: dependency cleanup failed\");\n735\t            return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n736\t        }\n737\t\n738\t        // Step 4: Agent log file cleanup.\n739\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_old_agent_logs(store, &mut summary).await {\n740\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: agent log cleanup failed\");\n741\t            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n742\t        }\n743\t\n744\t        // Step 4.5: Trace retention cleanup.\n745\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_old_traces(&mut summary) {\n746\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: trace cleanup failed\");\n747\t            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n748\t        }\n749\t\n750\t        // Step 4.75: Learning consolidation.\n751\t        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_learnings(&mut summary) {\n752\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: learning cleanup failed\");\n753\t            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n754\t        }\n755\t\n756\t        // Step 5: Database health check.\n757\t        if let Err(e) = self.check_db_health(store, &mut summary).await {\n758\t            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: database health check failed\");\n759\t            // DB check failure is non-fatal — continue with the summary.\n760\t        }\n761\t\n762\t        // Emit cycle summary telemetry.\n763\t        let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendCycleSummary {\n764\t            beads_released: summary.beads_released,\n765\t            locks_removed: summary.locks_removed,\n766\t            deps_cleaned: summary.deps_cleaned,\n767\t            db_repaired: summary.db_repaired,\n768\t            db_rebuilt: summary.db_rebuilt,\n769\t            agent_logs_cleaned: summary.agent_logs_cleaned,\n770\t            traces_pruned: summary.traces_pruned,\n771\t            traces_deleted: summary.traces_cleaned,\n772\t        });\n773\t\n774\t        if summary.did_work() {\n775\t            tracing::info!(\n776\t                beads_released = summary.beads_released,\n777\t                locks_removed = summary.locks_removed,\n778\t                deps_cleaned = summary.deps_cleaned,\n779\t                db_repaired = summary.db_repaired,\n780\t                db_rebuilt = summary.db_rebuilt,\n781\t                agent_logs_cleaned = summary.agent_logs_cleaned,\n782\t                traces_pruned = summary.traces_pruned,\n783\t                traces_deleted = summary.traces_cleaned,\n784\t                \"mend performed cleanup — restarting waterfall\"\n785\t            );\n786\t            StrandResult::WorkCreated\n787\t        } else {\n788\t            tracing::debug!(\"mend found nothing to clean\");\n789\t            StrandResult::NoWork\n790\t        }\n791\t    }\n792\t}\n793\t\n794\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n795\t// Helpers\n796\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n797\t\n798\t/// Get the age of a file based on its modification time.\n799\tfn file_age(path: &Path) -> Option<Duration> {\n800\t    let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path).ok()?;\n801\t    let modified = metadata.modified().ok()?;\n802\t    SystemTime::now().duration_since(modified).ok()\n803\t}\n804\t\n805\t/// Try to acquire an exclusive flock on a file (non-blocking).\n806\t///\n807\t/// Returns `Ok(Some(file))` if acquired, `Ok(None)` if held by another process.\n808\tfn try_acquire_flock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<std::fs::File>> {\n809\t    use fs2::FileExt;\n810\t\n811\t    let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n812\t        .read(true)\n813\t        .write(true)\n814\t        .open(path)?;\n815\t\n816\t    match file.try_lock_exclusive() {\n817\t        Ok(()) => Ok(Some(file)),\n818\t        Err(ref e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::WouldBlock => Ok(None),\n819\t        Err(e) => Err(e.into()),\n820\t    }\n821\t}\n822\t\n823\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n824\t// Tests\n825\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n826\t\n827\t#[cfg(test)]\n828\tmod tests {\n829\t    use super::*;\n830\t    use crate::bead_store::{Filters, use chrono::{TimeZone, Utc};\n836\t    use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, Ordering};\n837\t    use std::sync::Arc;\n838\t\n839\t    // ── Mock bead store ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n840\t\n841\t    struct MockBeadStore {\n842\t        all_beads: Vec<Bead>,\n843\t        release_count: Arc<AtomicU32>,\n844\t        /// If None, it fails.\n847\t        repair_report: Option<RepairReport>,\n848\t        /// Whether full_rebuild() should fail.\n849\t        rebuild_fails: bool,\n850\t    }\n851\t\n852\t    impl MockBeadStore {\n853\t        fn new(beads: Vec<Bead>) -> (Self, Arc<AtomicU32>) {\n854\t            let release_count = Arc::new(AtomicU32::new(0));\n855\t            (\n856\t                MockBeadStore {\n857\t                    all_beads: beads,\n858\t                    release_count: release_count.clone(),\n859\t                    check_warnings: vec![],\n860\t                    repair_report: Some(RepairReport::default()),\n861\t                    rebuild_fails: false,\n862\t                },\n863\t                release_count,\n864\t            )\n865\t        }\n866\t\n867\t        /// doctor_check returns warnings → escalates to repair (which succeeds).\n868\t        fn with_doctor_report(mut self, report: RepairReport) -> Self {\n869\t            self.check_warnings = report.warnings.clone();\n870\t            self.repair_report = Some(report);\n871\t            self\n872\t        }\n873\t\n874\t        /// doctor_check returns warnings, self.repair_report = None;\n878\t            self.rebuild_fails = false;\n879\t            self\n880\t        }\n881\t\n882\t        /// Everything fails → persistent corruption (ERRORED state).\n883\t        fn with_all_recovery_failure(mut self) -> Self {\n884\t            self.check_warnings = vec![\"corruption detected\".to_string()];\n885\t            self.repair_report = None;\n886\t            self.rebuild_fails = true;\n887\t            self\n888\t        }\n889\t    }\n890\t\n891\t    #[async_trait]\n892\t    impl BeadStore for MockBeadStore {\n893\t        async &BeadId) -> Result<()> {\n906\t            self.release_count.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);\n907\t            Ok(())\n908\t        }\n909\t        async fn flush(&self) -> Result<()> {\n910\t
